rulings.Case Title: MARGARITA VELIS VS. ABM JANITORIAL SERVICES, INC., A BUSINESS ENTITY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 23, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANT ABM JANITORIAL SERVICES - NORTHERN CALIFORNIA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for summary judgment denied. Motion for summary adjudication granted in part and denied in part. Motion granted as to harassment and failure to prevent harassment. No severe and pervasive conduct that altered the conditions of work. Motion granted for retaliation, no adverse employment action or nexus with protected activity. Motion denied as to failure to accommodate. No copy of FEHA claim attached to moving papers. Triable issues of material fact raised: whether plaintiff can perform the essential functions of her job without accommodation and the severity of plaintiff's condition; whether defendant engaged in the interactive process in good faith; whether defendant's accomodation of allowing days off was a reasonable accommodation. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN JAY SMITH VS. THE KARREN COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 23, 2009, line 5, DEFENDANT RON JOHNSON MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for Summary Judgment or in the alternative summary adjudication denied. Defendant fails to shift the burden of proof. Defendant fails to show he did not control the property. Defendant does not affirmatively negate an element of premises liability or negligence and fails to establish that plaintiff cannot prove a necessary element of his claims. To the contrary, defendant had extensive control over the bathroom, was given a Home Depot card for repair materials, was on-call to make repairs when tenants had issues, and had previously fixed a broken urinal. (Defendant's deposition.) The leaking urinal was a chronic problem. (Plaintiff's deposition 42:2-11.)=(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: WESTCORE MONTGOMERY, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED VS. WELLS FARGO BANK,N.A., A NATIONAL BANKING et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 23, 2009, line 13, CROSS COMPLAINANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A., MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion granted. "Rent" is specfically defined in Sec. 3.1 of the lease and does not inlcude "additional charges." The agreements consistently deal with these terms separately. A plain reading of Sec. 2.2.2 of the Second Lease amendment sets the last month's prior rent at $43.93 per sq. foot, and the "rent floor" component is $277,734.44. The Court does not consider extrinsic evidence. Westcore is bound by its Dec. 10, 2008 proposed rental rate in determining the prevailing market rent in arbitration. The lease first states that the parties may at any time engage in preliminary negotions regarding the rent, once the tenant has exercised the option to extend. (Sec. 2.2.2.2) The landlord has not later than 3 months to propose a formal written statement of the rental rate that the tenant can accept or elect to deliver a notice of arbitration that must contain the tenant's own good faith estimate of the prevailing rental rate. (Sec. 2.2.2.3) The arbitrators determine the actual prevailing market rent and "shall use" the closest of either the landlord's or the tenant's submitted proposed rental rate. The contract contemplates the parties will negotiate the terms of the renewed lease amicably, before resorting to arbitration. The contract also provides for the submission of specific written proposal and that each party is bound to its last estimate. The contract is designed to encourage both parties to be reasonable and act in good faith. Any ambiguity is construed against the drafting party, which in this case is landlord. (Civil Code sec. 1654). =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: WESTCORE MONTGOMERY, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED VS. WELLS FARGO BANK,N.A., A NATIONAL BANKING et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 23, 2009, line 13, PLAINTIFF WESTCORE MONTGOMERY, LLC, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
